goal: Build recovery/watchdog proof or design enough of it to enable future approval.
whatWasImplemented: Added a mock watchdog/recovery state machine with pending, confirmed, timeout, revert, and recovery-failure states.
Added a disabled high-risk recovery review model backed by the mock watchdog state.
Added a high-risk recovery workflow model with rollback proof requirements for backup, reread, timeout restore, and restore reread.
The recovery workflow records pending, confirmed, reverted, and recovery-failed states without enabling confirmation, revert, runtime, or writes.
Added deterministic tests for confirmation, timeout revert, and timeout recovery failure.
Recorded that high-risk/display-render production writes remain blocked.
Preserved family-specific recovery requirement language through active blocked copy tests.
Referenced existing high-risk family architecture as the current non-live proof basis.
Added a no-op live recovery readiness protocol for high-risk/display proof planning.
The protocol refuses real config paths, refuses runtime mutation requests, and records out-of-band recovery, dead-man timeout, verified restore, and explicit approval requirements.
Added deterministic tests proving the live-readiness harness remains no-op and does not accept real config or runtime mutation.
No additional production-safe high-risk/display step was available this sprint without crossing the live recovery proof and explicit approval boundary.
This sprint rechecked high-risk/display recovery and found no safe production step beyond the existing no-op protocol without explicit live recovery approval.
Controlled live-test guard requires out-of-band recovery and read-only runtime snapshot before high-risk/display mutation.
Added a guarded no-op live-readiness executor that requires guard approval, out-of-band recovery channel evidence, dead-man timeout evidence, and restore command evidence before reporting readiness.
Attempted read-only runtime socket checks for live-readiness context; socket was unavailable and no high-risk/display mutation was attempted.

Added a default-disabled high-risk/display production gate review requiring out-of-band recovery, dead-man timeout, restore command, config backup, runtime snapshot, explicit approval, and readiness proof.
Connected high-risk/display production gate review to the shared explicit approval-flow model; approval still blocks without out-of-band recovery and live restore proof.
whatWasNotImplemented: No real display/render writes were enabled.
No real reload or mutating hyprctl was used.
No live watchdog experiment was run.
blockers: Production high-risk writes require explicit user approval and live recovery proof.
Display/render failures need out-of-band rollback proof before activation.
The current live-readiness protocol is intentionally no-op and cannot mutate config or runtime.
nextRecommendedWork: Define a user-approved live proof protocol with out-of-band recovery before any high-risk/display production gate can be proposed.
